data.title: Expand Energy’s Earnings Call Signals Efficiency-Led Pivot
data.description:
Expand Energy Corporation ((EXE)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Expand Energy Corporation’s latest earnings call balanced confident operational achievements with candid discussion of risks. Management stressed a 15% reduction in Haynesville breakevens, strong hedging gains and rapid storage growth, while outlining a clear strategy to tap premium downstream markets. Transitional challenges, including leadership changes and a looming 2029 bond maturity, tempered but did not erase the overall constructive tone.

Haynesville Breakeven Reduction & Inventory Improvement

Expand Energy underscored a 15% drop in Haynesville breakevens over the past year, a key driver of better resilience in a volatile gas-price environment. The company also added roughly five years of inventory economic below $3.50 gas, which management says lowers future maintenance capital needs and strengthens downside protection for cash flows.

Hedging Gains

Risk management paid off as the company’s hedging program generated about $200 million in gains during the year. These gains cushioned the impact of commodity swings, supporting more stable cash generation and providing flexibility for both capital spending and debt reduction.

Storage Build-Out and Volatility Management

The company rapidly expanded owned gas storage, adding roughly 3.5 Bcf in the last quarter to reach about 5 Bcf, up from just 1.5 Bcf. Management said this capacity allows better capture of seasonal and regional price spikes and has already produced realized gains on storage-related trades.

Haynesville Productivity & Completion Improvements

Operational tweaks are lifting well performance, with first-year cumulative production expectations moving higher and 20% of 2025 TILs already exceeding 1 Bcf per 1,000 feet. The company expects that share to rise above 30% in 2026, driven by upgraded completion designs, higher proppant loading, in-house sand sourcing and longer laterals.

Production and CapEx Guidance with Improved Maintenance CapEx

For 2026, management is targeting about 7.5 Bcf per day of average production, with a range from 7.25 to 7.75 Bcf per day, funded by roughly $2.85 billion of capital spending. They highlighted that maintenance CapEx to sustain 7.5 Bcf per day has improved by about $225 million versus last year’s program, signaling better capital efficiency.

Large Market Opportunity & Premium-Market Optionality

The company framed its plan against a backdrop of robust gas demand growth, citing expectations for a 35%–40% rise in U.S. gas demand over five years and about 25 Bcf per day of new demand coming online. Greater connectivity to Gulf Coast hubs like Gillis and Perryville should increase access to premium pricing, enhancing realizations potential.

Targeted Realization Uplift

A central commercial ambition is to lift realizations by an incremental $0.20 per Mcf over a three-to-five-year horizon. Management equates that to roughly $500 million of additional EBITDA, framing it as a major value lever if the firm can consistently capture downstream and basis-related margin.

Balance Sheet Progress & Shareholder Returns

Management highlighted steady debt reduction over the year while still returning cash through dividends and share buybacks. The clear near-term priority is further deleveraging, but leadership signaled a willingness to continue opportunistic shareholder returns as conditions allow.

Operational Execution & Cost Reduction Initiatives

Across its basins, the company pointed to strong operational execution and an ongoing push to lower drilling and completion costs. Efforts include improving tool reliability, using AI-driven optimization and enhancing sourcing strategies, which management expects will drive further cost declines.

Strategic Commercial Shift

The call marked a strategic tilt toward more active marketing and downstream participation, including building a greater presence in Houston to be closer to key customers. The company aims to combine premium-market access, storage usage and broader commercial structures to capture more value beyond the wellhead.

Weather-Related Disruptions (Winter Storm FERN)

Operations faced weather-related setbacks as Winter Storm FERN brought more than an inch of ice to the Haynesville, disrupting power and water handling and cutting production in late January. Appalachia volumes were also temporarily curtailed before returning, contributing to quarter-to-quarter variability.

Commercial / Marketing Execution Lag

Despite its ambitions, management admitted that execution on securing long-term downstream demand deals has lagged. They said the team needs to be more aggressive in locking in premium contracts and offtake arrangements to fully monetize the company’s growing optionality.

Leadership Changes and CEO Search Uncertainty

The company announced senior leadership changes and signaled a CEO search expected to take roughly six to nine months. While management portrayed the transition as an opportunity, it introduces near-term uncertainty around strategic priorities and execution pace.

Competitive & Constrained Storage Market

Although the company has scaled its own storage footprint, management cautioned that the broader storage market is tight and highly competitive. Additional capacity is expensive and hard to secure quickly, limiting how fast the firm can expand this line of business.

Large 2029 Bond Maturity and Capital Allocation Pressure

Executives called the 2029 bond maturity a “big nut,” underscoring its importance in upcoming capital allocation decisions. Addressing this liability is shaping a strategy focused on balance-sheet repair, which may restrain the pace and size of future share repurchases.

M&A Pricing Environment & Selective Deal Activity

The company passed on several acquisition opportunities due to what it viewed as unattractive pricing, reflecting a frothy M&A landscape. Management suggested that inorganic growth will remain limited unless valuations reset or the company is willing to relax its discipline, which it currently is not.

Cash Tax Profile Will Shift Higher Over Time

The company currently enjoys minimal cash taxes thanks to OBDD-related benefits, supporting stronger near-term free cash flow. However, management expects to become a full cash taxpayer closer to 2030, implying higher cash tax outflows and a gradual drag on future free cash generation.

Forward-Looking Guidance and Strategic Outlook

Looking ahead, the company guided to about 7.5 Bcf per day of 2026 production, supported by roughly $2.85 billion in CapEx and an efficient program up to around 7.75 Bcf per day. Management reiterated mid-cycle gas price assumptions of $3.50–$4.00, highlighted sustained improvements in Haynesville breakevens and well productivity, and emphasized plans to reduce debt, modestly trim D&C costs and pursue commercial initiatives aimed at achieving the $0.20 per Mcf realizations uplift.

Expand Energy’s earnings call painted a picture of a gas producer leaning hard into efficiency gains, storage and premium-market access while staying disciplined on leverage and deals. Investors must weigh strong operational and financial progress, plus a sizable realizations opportunity, against leadership transition risk, a demanding storage and M&A backdrop and the need to tackle the sizeable 2029 bond.

data.title: Lottery Corporation Earnings Call Highlights Resilient Core
data.description:
Lottery Corporation Limited ((AU:TLC)) has held its Q2 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Lottery Corporation Limited’s latest earnings call struck a tone of resilience in the face of unusually weak jackpot conditions. Management emphasized stable revenue and EBITDA, strong cash generation and a maintained dividend, while acknowledging headwinds from softer jackpots, lower active customers and regulatory uncertainty that are tempering near‑term momentum but not derailing the long‑term strategy.

Resilient Revenue Base

Group revenue held at $1.8 billion in H1 FY26, underscoring the benefits of a diversified portfolio across games and channels. This breadth helped cushion the impact of the leanest jackpot run since listing, with non‑jackpot products and broader game mix supporting overall turnover.

Stable EBITDA and Profitability

EBITDA came in at $367 million, slipping just 0.7% year on year, while net profit after tax declined a modest 1.4%. The limited earnings deterioration, despite significant jackpot headwinds, signals a business model that can absorb volatility without severe margin compression.

Maintained Dividend and Strong Cash Flow

The board maintained an interim fully franked dividend of $0.08 per share, matching the prior period and equating to 103% of first‑half net profit. Management argued this high payout remains sustainable due to robust free cash flow generation and balance sheet flexibility.

Digital Traction

Digital lottery turnover rose to 41.2% of total, with online sales continuing to climb through the half. Management positioned this digital shift as a structural margin driver and an avenue for richer customer engagement that can help smooth future jackpot cycles.

Keno and Instant Scratch‑Its Outperformance

Keno delivered a record half, with retail turnover up 7% and online Keno up 3.5%, highlighting strong demand in both channels. Instant Scratch‑Its also impressed, posting 8.5% sales growth in key gifting periods and demonstrating the appeal of smaller, more frequent win formats.

Successful Saturday Lotto Refresh

The Saturday Lotto refresh, with a $6 million Division 1 offer and price increase, is showing encouraging early traction. After 29 weeks, price retention sits at 103%, meaning customers have largely absorbed the change and are delivering incremental revenue per draw.

Strong Balance Sheet and Liquidity

Net debt to EBITDA stands at around 3 times, the bottom of the 3–4 times target range, supported by $560 million in available liquidity. With an average debt tenor of roughly 4.5 years and about 85% of borrowings fixed or hedged, the company retains ample room for disciplined investment or capital returns.

Disciplined Cost and Capital Framework

Operating expenses rose 2.9% to $146 million in the half, with full‑year OpEx guided to $310–$320 million as management keeps growth below normalized revenue. CapEx was $34 million in H1, with a FY26 target of $90–$100 million focused on digital transformation and retail network upgrades.

Unusually Weak Jackpot Environment

The first half was marked by the weakest jackpot environment since listing, with Division 1 offers across jackpot games down 14% on the prior period. This scarcity of large jackpots dampened player excitement and participation, especially in headline draw games that typically drive spikes in turnover.

Material Turnover Impact from Jackpots

Management estimated that weaker jackpots created about a $400 million unfavorable turnover impact in H1 FY26 versus statistical norms. This compares with an already negative $200 million impact in the prior corresponding half, underscoring the scale of the current jackpot shortfall.

Jackpot‑Driven Earnings Drag

The jackpot volatility translated into an approximate $26 million EBITDA headwind versus the previous year. This drag explains much of the modest decline in both EBITDA and net profit, reinforcing how sensitive earnings are to the cadence and size of major jackpots.

Active Customer Decline

Active customers slipped to 8.6 million over the half, which management largely attributed to customers lapsing in the absence of frequent large jackpots. Executives stressed that core registered customers appear sticky, suggesting the decline is cyclical rather than evidence of structural churn.

Powerball Softness and Pricing Nuances

Powerball underperformed on a like‑for‑like basis, reflecting both jackpot weakness and some sensitivity to recent price changes. The latest Powerball price rise has retained around 61% of the 17% increase so far, but management cautioned that true retention will only be clear once jackpot frequency normalizes.

Regulatory and License Risk

The call flagged ongoing regulatory and competitive risks, including a major licence expiring in 2028 that introduces medium‑term uncertainty. Management also highlighted foreign matched lottery products operating under different rules, arguing they create competitive distortion and add to the regulatory overhang.

Second‑Half Cost and Investment Phasing

Both OpEx and CapEx are expected to be weighted to the second half, reflecting higher advertising, technology spend and project execution. While this back‑ended investment supports long‑term growth, it could temporarily pressure margins if jackpot activity does not revert towards historical averages.

Forward‑Looking Guidance and Strategic Priorities

Management reiterated FY26 guidance parameters, including OpEx of $310–$320 million and CapEx of $90–$100 million, with spending ramping in H2. The company aims to keep cost growth below normalized revenue growth, leverage its 41.2% digital share and strong Keno and Instant Scratch‑Its momentum, and will outline further strategy at a mid‑year Investor Day.

Lottery Corporation’s earnings call painted a picture of a business navigating cyclical jackpot headwinds with a resilient base and a clear digital and cost agenda. For investors, the key watchpoints will be jackpot mean reversion, customer re‑engagement and regulatory outcomes, set against a solid balance sheet and an unswerving commitment to shareholder returns.

data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

The latest announcement is out from Kinross Gold ( (TSE:K) ).

On February 18, 2026, Kinross Gold furnished a Form 6-K to the U.S. SEC providing its audited consolidated financial statements and Management’s Discussion and Analysis for the year ended December 31, 2025. The filing, which includes auditor consents, formally updates U.S. investors on Kinross’s 2025 financial and operating performance and incorporates these disclosures into its existing U.S. registration statements, underscoring ongoing compliance and transparency for securities holders.

The 2025 MD&A outlines Kinross’s results and risk profile in the context of volatile gold, silver and input costs, as well as foreign-exchange and political risks in key operating jurisdictions. By detailing segment performance across its mines in the Americas, Mauritania and Finland, the documents give investors an integrated view of production trends, cost structure and exposure factors that influence profitability and cash flow in the gold mining industry.

data.title: JFB Construction Raises Capital Amid AI Robotics Merger
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

JFB Construction Holdings Class A ( (JFB) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On February 13, 2026, JFB Construction Holdings signed a merger agreement with Xtend Reality Expansion and newly formed Xtend AI Robotics, Inc., under which an Israeli shell subsidiary will merge into Xtend and a second subsidiary will then merge into JFB, leaving both Xtend and JFB as wholly owned units of Newco. The deal structure will give Xtend shareholders at least 70.5% of Newco and JFB stockholders about 19.9%, with detailed provisions to roll over JFB and Xtend equity into Newco stock and options, plus up to 20 million additional Newco shares as earnout for Xtend investors tied to 2026 and 2027 results.

The transactions are targeted to close in mid‑2026, subject to extensive regulatory clearances, stockholder approvals and a $110 million minimum cash condition that is expected to be met largely via warrant exercises, while a series of support agreements lock in key shareholders on both sides and restrict post‑closing share transfers for 180 days. Alongside the merger signing, JFB completed a private placement on February 18, 2026, raising about $9.2 million in net proceeds from institutional investors, bolstering liquidity for the Xtend SAFE investment and underscoring investor backing for the shift toward an AI‑robotics‑focused public platform.

More about JFB Construction Holdings Class A

JFB Construction Holdings Class A operates as a publicly traded holding company that is now positioning itself as the corporate vehicle for a proposed combination with Xtend Reality Expansion Ltd., an AI-driven robotics and extended‑reality technology firm. The merged structure will be organized under a new Delaware corporation, Xtend AI Robotics, Inc. (“Newco”), with Newco seeking a Nasdaq listing under the ticker “XTND.”

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $182.7M

Willi Food Inte ( (WILC) ) has shared an update.

G. Willi-Food International has called a Special General Meeting of shareholders for March 24, 2026 in Yavne, Israel, according to a Form 6-K filed on February 18, 2026. Shareholders of record as of February 24, 2026 will be eligible to participate and vote, either in person or by proxy, with a quorum set at 25% of outstanding ordinary shares.

On the agenda are three key governance matters: approval of the terms of office for Mrs. Karin Williger as head of business optimization and business development effective January 1, 2026, a three-year extension of the services agreement with controlling shareholder Willi-Food Investments Ltd. from January 1, 2026, and the election of Ms. Michal Fishler Mund as an external director for a three-year term starting June 1, 2026. These votes could shape the company’s leadership structure and its relationship with its controlling shareholder, with special approval thresholds applying to related-party and external director items under Israeli corporate law.

The latest announcement is out from VCI Global Limited ( (VCIG) ).

On February 18, 2026, VCI Global said its blockchain arm Smart Bridge Technologies signed a definitive partnership agreement with Mezzofy Holding to build a Real-World Asset digital coupon exchange focused on tokenized vouchers. The structure gives Smart Bridge 51% control of the planned entity and regulatory and settlement leadership, while Mezzofy contributes its technology stack and multinational merchant network, aiming to plug into an existing enterprise ecosystem rather than rely on speculative retail demand.

The move marks a shift from development to commercial deployment in VCI Global’s digital asset strategy, positioning the exchange as a core infrastructure pillar expected to generate transaction fees, enterprise tokenization services and settlement revenues. By targeting the large but fragmented global digital voucher market and enabling secondary trading and price discovery for previously illiquid vouchers, the initiative could enhance VCI Global’s role as an early infrastructure provider in enterprise-focused digital commerce, though final capital commitments and the actual formation of the joint entity remain subject to further agreements and approvals.

data.title: SMJ International Says No Undisclosed News Behind NYSE Trading Spike
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SMJ International Holdings Inc. Class A ( (SMJF) ) just unveiled an update.

SMJ International Holdings Inc. is a Singapore-based premier flooring specialist serving commercial and institutional customers across Asia. It distributes carpet tiles, broadloom carpets and vinyl tiles under its proprietary SMJ brand in Singapore and over 20 mainly Asian markets, and offers environmentally certified flooring products aligned with regional sustainable building initiatives.

On February 18, 2026, SMJ International Holdings Inc. disclosed that it had observed unusual trading activity in its common stock on the NYSE American following market action on February 17, 2026. After making inquiries, the company stated it was not aware of any undisclosed material information or new business developments that would explain the trading, signalling to investors that the volatility did not stem from known changes in its operations or strategy.

More about SMJ International Holdings Inc. Class A

SMJ International Holdings Inc. is a Singapore-based premier flooring specialist serving commercial and institutional sectors across Asia. The company sells and distributes a wide range of premium flooring products, including carpet tiles, broadloom carpets and vinyl tiles, under its proprietary SMJ brand in Singapore and more than 20 primarily Asian markets.

Gladstone Investment ( (GAIN) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Gladstone Investment Corporation has issued up to $115 million of 7.125% unsecured notes due May 1, 2031, under a supplemental indenture executed with UMB Bank on February 18, 2026. The notes, which closed the same day, rank pari passu with the company’s existing unsecured debt, pay interest quarterly beginning May 1, 2026, and are redeemable at par on or after May 1, 2028.

Proceeds from the offering will be used to repay a portion of borrowings under Gladstone Investment’s credit facility, fund new investment opportunities and for general corporate purposes, with the company planning to re-borrow under the facility to continue investing in portfolio companies as conditions permit. The indenture also imposes covenants tying leverage and reporting obligations to Investment Company Act standards, providing noteholders with structural protections while supporting the firm’s ongoing growth strategy in the lower middle-market financing space.
